title: Generalized terminal-op direct output
hypothesis: The accepted direct terminal-output mechanism can be generalized beyond terminal Softmax to a small bounded terminal-op family on the existing nm1 hardware, but the first proof should be staged as measurement-only non-fused references followed by paired comparison on those same points.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Can a bounded mapper generalization extend direct terminal output beyond Softmax while preserving a clean measurement-first proof on fixed nm1 architecture points?
include: measurement-only non-fused nm1 reference points on a minimal generalized terminal-op suite
paired direct-output vs non-fused nm1 comparison on the same terminal-op suite
exclude: broad architecture sweeps before terminal-op legality and local gain are established
broad non-MLP model support claims beyond the chosen first-pass terminal-op family
follow_on_broad_sweep: run broader ranking or broader model-support evaluation only if the bounded generalized terminal-op comparison is positive or mixed enough to justify reintegration

expected_benefit: extends the accepted direct-output mechanism beyond a softmax-only rule
keeps the next proof focused on mapper legality and lowering quality instead of architecture ranking noise
uses measurement-only first-stage evaluation so reference points can be recorded without forcing a premature proposal judgment
risks: the chosen terminal-op family may still be too close to the existing softmax-tail proof
quality-gate requirements may grow if the generalized path touches numerically sensitive ops
a later broader model-support item may still be needed even if the first generalized mapper proof succeeds

expected_result.reason: the first item should only record fixed-point non-fused nm1 reference metrics for the chosen bounded terminal-op suite
if the generalized direct-output lowering is valid, it should improve at least part of the bounded terminal-op suite against the refreshed non-fused references
